cat_sub21_sub21_sub12.name: API & Integration Platforms
cat_sub21_sub21_sub12.description: Services connecting AI models to API management systems, integration platforms, and service orchestration tools.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub12.boundary: Not for single-application integrations, standalone tools, or non-API-based connection methods.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub12.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates AI with API management, multi-service integration, or platform connectivity systems, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub21_sub16.name: Social & Community
cat_sub21_sub21_sub16.description: Services connecting AI models to social platforms, community forums, and interactive discussion systems.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub16.boundary: Not for private messaging, team collaboration, or non-social information platforms.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub16.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates AI with social networks, community platforms, or public discussion forums, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub21_sub2.name: Design & Creative Tools
cat_sub21_sub21_sub2.description: Services connecting AI models to design platforms, creative software, and visual content creation tools.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub2.boundary: Not for general productivity software, document editors, or non-creative development tools.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates AI with design systems, creative workflows, or visual content generation, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub21_sub20.name: Utilities & Tools
cat_sub21_sub21_sub20.description: Services connecting AI models to utility applications, specialized tools, and functional helper platforms.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub20.boundary: Not for domain-specific applications, industry platforms, or comprehensive business systems.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub20.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates AI with utility functions, specialized tools, or helper applications without a specific domain focus, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub21_sub3.name: Development & Engineering
cat_sub21_sub21_sub3.description: Services for AI integration with software development tools, code repositories, and engineering workflows.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub3.boundary: Not for general productivity tools, business software, or non-technical creative applications.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates AI with coding, software engineering, or technical development processes, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub21_sub4.name: Database & Data Management
cat_sub21_sub21_sub4.description: Services connecting AI models to databases, data storage systems, and structured data management platforms.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub4.boundary: Not for file storage, document management, or unstructured data processing without database integration.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates AI with database operations, data querying, or structured data management, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub21_sub5.name: Communication & Collaboration
cat_sub21_sub21_sub5.description: Services for AI integration with team communication platforms, messaging systems, and collaborative tools.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub5.boundary: Not for individual productivity tools, personal assistants, or non-collaborative communication services.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub5.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates AI with team communication, collaborative workspaces, or group messaging platforms, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub21_sub6.name: Content Management Systems
cat_sub21_sub21_sub6.description: Services connecting AI models to CMS platforms for content creation, management, and publishing workflows.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub6.boundary: Not for document editors, file storage systems, or non-CMS content creation tools.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub6.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates AI with content management systems, publishing platforms, or editorial workflows, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub21_sub7.name: Knowledge & Information
cat_sub21_sub21_sub7.description: Services for AI integration with knowledge bases, information repositories, and research platforms.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub7.boundary: Not for general web search, social media, or real-time information services.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub7.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates AI with knowledge bases, information retrieval systems, or research databases, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub21_sub8.name: Productivity & Organization
cat_sub21_sub21_sub8.description: Services connecting AI models to personal productivity tools, task management systems, and organizational platforms.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub8.boundary: Not for team collaboration tools, enterprise systems, or specialized professional software.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub8.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates AI with personal productivity, task management, or individual organization tools, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub21_sub9.name: Hardware & IoT Integration
cat_sub21_sub21_sub9.description: Services for AI integration with physical devices, IoT systems, and hardware control platforms.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub9.boundary: Not for software-only systems, virtual environments, or cloud services without hardware interaction.
cat_sub21_sub21_sub9.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates AI with physical hardware, IoT devices, or embedded systems, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub4_sub1.name: Application Testing
cat_sub21_sub4_sub1.description: Services for automating functional tests on web, mobile, or desktop applications.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub1.boundary: Focuses on user interaction and functional validation of applications through their user interface.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily automates testing of applications through their UI (browser, mobile OS, or desktop OS), classify here. For web apps, this includes browser automation. For mobile, includes native/hybrid apps. For desktop, includes native OS applications.

cat_sub21_sub4_sub10.name: AI & ML System Testing
cat_sub21_sub4_sub10.description: Services for testing artificial intelligence systems and machine learning models.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub10.boundary: Specific to validating behavior, accuracy, and robustness of AI/ML systems.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub10.decision_rule: If the service primarily automates testing of AI systems, ML models, or intelligent agents, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub4_sub12.name: Specialized Domain Testing
cat_sub21_sub4_sub12.description: Services for testing in specialized domains like games, data pipelines, or infrastructure.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub12.boundary: Covers testing of domain-specific systems beyond standard applications.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub12.decision_rule: If the service primarily automates testing of specialized systems (games, data pipelines, ETL processes, infrastructure, DevOps pipelines), classify here.

cat_sub21_sub4_sub2.name: API & Service Testing
cat_sub21_sub4_sub2.description: Services for testing APIs, microservices, and backend services through direct network calls.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub2.boundary: Focuses on programmatic interaction with service endpoints without UI interaction.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily automates testing of APIs, web services, or microservices through network protocols (REST, GraphQL, gRPC, SOAP), classify here.

cat_sub21_sub4_sub3.name: Performance & Load Testing
cat_sub21_sub4_sub3.description: Services for load, stress, and performance testing of applications and infrastructure.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub3.boundary: Measures system behavior under load conditions (response times, throughput, stability).
cat_sub21_sub4_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily automates performance, load, stress, or scalability testing, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub4_sub4.name: Visual & UI Testing
cat_sub21_sub4_sub4.description: Services for visual validation, screenshot comparison, and UI component testing.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub4.boundary: Focuses on visual appearance, consistency, and regression detection of user interfaces.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily automates visual comparison, screenshot validation, or UI component testing, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub4_sub6.name: Cloud & Cross-Platform Testing
cat_sub21_sub4_sub6.description: Services for testing using cloud infrastructure across multiple platforms/browsers.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub6.boundary: Leverages cloud-based execution with parallel testing across environments.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub6.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides testing through cloud infrastructure with parallel execution across platforms/browsers/devices, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub4_sub7.name: Test Development & Analysis
cat_sub21_sub4_sub7.description: Services for creating test artifacts, debugging, and analyzing test results.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub7.boundary: Focuses on test creation, debugging, and result analysis rather than execution.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub7.decision_rule: If the service primarily generates test cases/scripts/data, provides debugging/observability, or analyzes test results, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub4_sub8.name: Test Orchestration & Integration
cat_sub21_sub4_sub8.description: Services for managing test execution, integrating with frameworks, and workflow coordination.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub8.boundary: Focuses on test management, framework integration, and workflow orchestration.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub8.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages test execution, integrates with test frameworks/tools, or orchestrates testing workflows, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub4_sub9.name: End-to-End & Workflow Testing
cat_sub21_sub4_sub9.description: Services for testing complete user workflows across multiple systems.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub9.boundary: Tests integrated flows spanning multiple application layers and systems.
cat_sub21_sub4_sub9.decision_rule: If the service primarily automates testing of complete, multi-step workflows across system boundaries (UI, API, database), classify here.

cat_sub21_sub8_sub1.name: Content & Website Platforms
cat_sub21_sub8_sub1.description: Services for interacting with CMS platforms, website builders, or design tools for content and site management.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub1.boundary: Covers traditional CMS (WordPress), visual website builders (Webflow), and design platforms (Figma).
cat_sub21_sub8_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables interaction with a platform for creating, managing, or designing website content or structure, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub8_sub10.name: Application Development
cat_sub21_sub8_sub10.description: Services for building, deploying, or managing server-side applications with specific frameworks.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub10.boundary: Includes framework-specific tools (Quarkus, .NET) and general development utilities/sandboxes.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub10.decision_rule: If the service primarily assists in building, deploying, or coding applications (server-side or general utilities), classify here.

cat_sub21_sub8_sub11.name: Integration & Gateway Platforms
cat_sub21_sub8_sub11.description: Services that act as hubs to connect with numerous third-party APIs, handling unified access.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub11.boundary: Covers API aggregation platforms, unified gateways, and multi-service connectors.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub11.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides a unified gateway or platform to connect with many external APIs, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub8_sub14.name: AI & Machine Learning Services
cat_sub21_sub8_sub14.description: Services for accessing AI models, machine learning APIs, or intelligent processing capabilities.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub14.boundary: Includes language models, computer vision APIs, predictive analytics, and AI agent platforms.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub14.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides access to AI models, ML capabilities, or intelligent processing, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub8_sub2.name: Business & Commerce Platforms
cat_sub21_sub8_sub2.description: Services for integrating with platforms handling business operations, commerce, payments, or CRM.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub2.boundary: Includes e-commerce backends, payment processors, project management tools, and CRM systems.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates with a platform for business operations, financial transactions, or customer management, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub8_sub4.name: Data & Storage Systems
cat_sub21_sub8_sub4.description: Services for querying, managing, or interacting with databases, file storage, or data formats.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub4.boundary: Includes SQL/NoSQL databases, graph databases, file storage APIs, and data format (JSON/XML) processors.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides direct access to manage, query, or transform structured data or storage, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub8_sub6.name: API Lifecycle Management
cat_sub21_sub8_sub6.description: Services for governing API gateways, analytics, security, and developer portals.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub6.boundary: Focuses on managing published APIs, not designing or testing them.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub6.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages API gateways, tokens, analytics, or portals for live APIs, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub8_sub7.name: API Design & Specification
cat_sub21_sub8_sub7.description: Services for creating, validating, and managing API definition formats like OpenAPI or GraphQL schemas.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub7.boundary: Includes OpenAPI/Swagger tools, GraphQL schema utilities, and API description validators.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub7.decision_rule: If the service primarily works with API specification documents for design, validation, or exploration, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub8_sub8.name: API Testing & Debugging
cat_sub21_sub8_sub8.description: Services focused on executing, validating, and debugging calls to API endpoints.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub8.boundary: Covers API call execution, collection runners, performance testing, and debugging tools.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub8.decision_rule: If the service primarily executes, validates, or debugs API calls and collections, classify here.

cat_sub21_sub8_sub9.name: SDK & Client Generation
cat_sub21_sub8_sub9.description: Services that automatically produce client libraries or code snippets from API specifications.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub9.boundary: Focuses on generating consumer-side code, SDKs, or type definitions.
cat_sub21_sub8_sub9.decision_rule: If the service primarily generates client-side code, SDKs, or types from API specs, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub1_sub1.name: General-Purpose Relational Databases
cat_sub22_sub1_sub1.description: Managed services for traditional SQL databases supporting transactional and operational workloads.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub1.boundary: Includes managed MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQL Server, Oracle. Excludes analytical warehouses, in-memory caches, or specialized engines.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides a managed standard SQL database for general application data storage and transactions, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub1_sub10.name: Database Administration & Operations
cat_sub22_sub1_sub10.description: Tools for managing, monitoring, optimizing, and interacting with databases.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub10.boundary: Includes schema migration tools, performance monitors, query editors, admin consoles. Excludes data governance or integration platforms.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub10.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides tools for database administration, monitoring, querying, or operational management, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub1_sub11.name: Data Management & Governance
cat_sub22_sub1_sub11.description: Services for data integration, cataloging, lineage, security, and pipeline orchestration.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub11.boundary: Includes data catalogs, ETL/ELT tools, metadata managers, security controls. Excludes database administration or query engines.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub11.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides data integration, cataloging, lineage, security, or pipeline orchestration capabilities, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub1_sub12.name: Database Connectivity & Drivers
cat_sub22_sub1_sub12.description: Interfaces, drivers, and layers for connecting applications to database systems.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub12.boundary: Includes ODBC, JDBC, database drivers, connection pools. Excludes database engines or administration tools.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub12.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides a connectivity layer, driver, or interface for accessing databases from applications, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub1_sub13.name: Serverless & Embedded Databases
cat_sub22_sub1_sub13.description: Databases designed for serverless functions, edge deployment, or embedded execution.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub13.boundary: Includes SQLite cloud services, edge databases, databases scaling to zero. Excludes traditional managed databases or BaaS platforms.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub13.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages a database designed for serverless, edge, or embedded deployment with minimal operational overhead, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub1_sub14.name: Database Infrastructure & Deployment
cat_sub22_sub1_sub14.description: Services for deploying, configuring, and managing database infrastructure and runtimes.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub14.boundary: Includes database deployment tools, control panels, infrastructure automation. Excludes database engines or administration interfaces.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub14.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides tools for deploying, configuring, or managing database infrastructure and runtime environments, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub1_sub2.name: Analytical Data Warehousing
cat_sub22_sub1_sub2.description: Services for columnar databases optimized for complex queries and large-scale business intelligence.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub2.boundary: Includes cloud data warehouses like Snowflake, Redshift, BigQuery. Excludes real-time analytics engines or operational databases.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages a database platform designed for batch analytical and business intelligence workloads, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub1_sub3.name: Real-Time Analytics Engines
cat_sub22_sub1_sub3.description: Databases built for low-latency analytical queries on streaming or rapidly changing data.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub3.boundary: Includes ClickHouse, Druid, Pinot. Excludes batch warehouses or operational relational databases.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages a database optimized for sub-second analytical queries on real-time data streams, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub1_sub4.name: In-Memory Data Stores & Caching
cat_sub22_sub1_sub4.description: Services for key-value stores and data structures held primarily in RAM for fast access.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub4.boundary: Includes Redis, Memcached, in-memory databases. Excludes persistent operational databases or analytical engines.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages an in-memory data store used for caching, session storage, or fast ephemeral data access, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub1_sub5.name: Specialized Database Engines
cat_sub22_sub1_sub5.description: Services for databases optimized for specific data models like graph, time-series, or document.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub5.boundary: Includes graph databases (Neo4j), time-series databases (InfluxDB), document databases (MongoDB). Excludes general-purpose relational or analytical warehouses.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub5.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages a database designed for a specific data model (graph, time-series, document, etc.), classify here.

cat_sub22_sub1_sub7.name: Backend-as-a-Service (BaaS)
cat_sub22_sub1_sub7.description: Services providing databases as part of a full-stack, serverless backend platform.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub7.boundary: Includes Firebase, Supabase, Appwrite. Excludes standalone database services or generic cloud platforms.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub7.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides a database integrated with authentication, serverless functions, and other app backend services, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub1_sub8.name: Cloud Database Platforms
cat_sub22_sub1_sub8.description: Managed platforms for deploying and operating multiple database technologies.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub8.boundary: Includes Aiven, ScaleGrid, Render database services. Excludes proprietary single-cloud services or BaaS platforms.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub8.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides a managed multi-cloud platform for deploying and operating various database instances, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub1_sub9.name: Cloud Provider Database Services
cat_sub22_sub1_sub9.description: Proprietary managed database offerings native to major cloud providers.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub9.boundary: Includes Amazon RDS, Azure SQL Database, Google Cloud SQL. Excludes third-party platforms or multi-cloud services.
cat_sub22_sub1_sub9.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages a proprietary database service offered by a major public cloud provider (AWS, Azure, GCP), classify here.

cat_sub22_sub19_sub1.name: Infrastructure & Cloud
cat_sub22_sub19_sub1.description: Services for managing cloud infrastructure, compute resources, and platform-as-a-service operations.
cat_sub22_sub19_sub1.boundary: Excludes source code management (see Development & DevOps) and data-specific platforms (see Data & Analytics).
cat_sub22_sub19_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages cloud compute, storage, or infrastructure resources, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub19_sub18.name: MCP Infrastructure
cat_sub22_sub19_sub18.description: Services for MCP protocol management, server orchestration, and MCP ecosystem tooling.
cat_sub22_sub19_sub18.boundary: Excludes application-specific MCP servers (see other categories) and general infrastructure (see Infrastructure & Cloud).
cat_sub22_sub19_sub18.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages MCP servers, routing, or protocol infrastructure, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub19_sub2.name: Development & DevOps
cat_sub22_sub19_sub2.description: Services for source code management, CI/CD pipelines, build systems, and developer tooling.
cat_sub22_sub19_sub2.boundary: Excludes infrastructure provisioning (see Infrastructure & Cloud) and application monitoring (see Observability).
cat_sub22_sub19_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily handles source code repositories, builds, or developer workflows, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub19_sub3.name: Data & Analytics
cat_sub22_sub19_sub3.description: Services for data processing, analytics platforms, data warehousing, and business intelligence.
cat_sub22_sub19_sub3.boundary: Excludes message streaming (see Event Streaming) and infrastructure monitoring (see Observability).
cat_sub22_sub19_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily processes, analyzes, or warehouses business data, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub19_sub4.name: Event Streaming
cat_sub22_sub19_sub4.description: Services for managing real-time data streams, message brokers, and event-driven architectures.
cat_sub22_sub19_sub4.boundary: Excludes batch data processing (see Data & Analytics) and API gateways (see API Management).
cat_sub22_sub19_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily handles real-time message streaming or pub/sub systems, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub19_sub5.name: Observability
cat_sub22_sub19_sub5.description: Services for monitoring, logging, tracing, and performance analysis of applications and systems.
cat_sub22_sub19_sub5.boundary: Excludes infrastructure management (see Infrastructure & Cloud) and security monitoring (see Security & IAM).
cat_sub22_sub19_sub5.decision_rule: If the service primarily monitors application performance, logs, or metrics, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub19_sub6.name: Security & IAM
cat_sub22_sub19_sub6.description: Services for identity management, authentication, authorization, and security policy enforcement.
cat_sub22_sub19_sub6.boundary: Excludes infrastructure security (see Infrastructure & Cloud) and API security (see API Management).
cat_sub22_sub19_sub6.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages user identities, access control, or authentication, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub19_sub7.name: API Management
cat_sub22_sub19_sub7.description: Services for API gateways, routing, rate limiting, and API lifecycle management.
cat_sub22_sub19_sub7.boundary: Excludes general infrastructure (see Infrastructure & Cloud) and security services (see Security & IAM).
cat_sub22_sub19_sub7.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages API gateways, routing, or API policies, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub19_sub8.name: Database & Storage
cat_sub22_sub19_sub8.description: Services for database operations, object storage, and persistent data management.
cat_sub22_sub19_sub8.boundary: Excludes data analytics (see Data & Analytics) and file sharing (see Collaboration).
cat_sub22_sub19_sub8.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides database operations or object storage, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub3_sub1.name: Application Performance Monitoring
cat_sub22_sub3_sub1.description: Services for monitoring application performance, errors, crashes, user experience, and API/developer tool health.
cat_sub22_sub3_sub1.boundary: Focuses on application-level performance, errors, crashes, user experience (APM, RUM), and API/developer toolchain monitoring.
cat_sub22_sub3_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily monitors application-level performance, errors, crashes, user experience, API performance, or developer tool health, classify here. NOT for infrastructure/system metrics, log/trace analysis, or business metrics.

cat_sub22_sub3_sub12.name: Observability Platform & Integration
cat_sub22_sub3_sub12.description: Services providing unified access to observability data, integrations, and IT asset/inventory management.
cat_sub22_sub3_sub12.boundary: Focuses on multi-data-type observability platforms, tool integrations, and IT asset/inventory tracking.
cat_sub22_sub3_sub12.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides unified access/integration across multiple observability data types or tools, or tracks IT assets/inventory, classify here. NOT for specialized single-data-type monitoring or comprehensive platforms that should be classified in specific data-type categories (e.g., APM, Logging).

cat_sub22_sub3_sub2.name: Infrastructure Monitoring
cat_sub22_sub3_sub2.description: Services for monitoring servers, VMs, cloud resources, hardware, and endpoint/device health and performance.
cat_sub22_sub3_sub2.boundary: Focuses on compute, storage, network resource health, and individual device/endpoint performance at the infrastructure layer.
cat_sub22_sub3_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily monitors server, VM, cloud resource, hardware, desktop, endpoint, or IoT device health and metrics (CPU, memory, disk), classify here. NOT for application performance, container orchestration, or cloud cost data.

cat_sub22_sub3_sub3.name: Log Analysis
cat_sub22_sub3_sub3.description: Services for collecting, indexing, searching, and analyzing log data from applications and systems.
cat_sub22_sub3_sub3.boundary: Focuses on unstructured or semi-structured log data ingestion, querying, and analysis.
cat_sub22_sub3_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily ingests, queries, and analyzes log data, classify here. NOT for structured metrics, distributed traces, or observability platform integrations.

cat_sub22_sub3_sub4.name: Distributed Tracing
cat_sub22_sub3_sub4.description: Services for collecting, visualizing, and analyzing traces to monitor request flows across distributed systems.
cat_sub22_sub3_sub4.boundary: Focuses on trace and span data for performance analysis of distributed transactions.
cat_sub22_sub3_sub4.decision_rule: If the service is primarily a trace-centric platform for managing and querying distributed traces and spans, classify here. NOT for log analysis, application error monitoring, or APM tools that include tracing as a secondary feature.

cat_sub22_sub3_sub5.name: Network & Connectivity Monitoring
cat_sub22_sub3_sub5.description: Services for monitoring network devices, traffic, connectivity, website availability, and external service status.
cat_sub22_sub3_sub5.boundary: Focuses on network infrastructure, external connectivity, and service availability.
cat_sub22_sub3_sub5.decision_rule: If the service primarily monitors network devices, traffic, connectivity, website availability, or third-party service uptime, classify here. NOT for internal application performance or server infrastructure metrics.

cat_sub22_sub3_sub8.name: Development Pipeline Monitoring
cat_sub22_sub3_sub8.description: Services for monitoring CI/CD pipelines, build status, deployment processes, and test execution.
cat_sub22_sub3_sub8.boundary: Focuses on development and deployment pipeline health and performance.
cat_sub22_sub3_sub8.decision_rule: If the service primarily monitors CI/CD pipelines, builds, deployments, or test execution, classify here. NOT for production application monitoring or infrastructure health.

cat_sub22_sub3_sub9.name: AI/ML & Data Pipeline Monitoring
cat_sub22_sub3_sub9.description: Services for monitoring machine learning pipelines, model performance, data pipelines, and data processing jobs.
cat_sub22_sub3_sub9.boundary: Focuses on AI/ML workflows and data processing pipeline health.
cat_sub22_sub3_sub9.decision_rule: If the service primarily monitors ML pipelines, model performance, data pipelines, or data processing jobs, classify here. NOT for general application monitoring or CI/CD pipelines.

cat_sub22_sub5_sub1.name: Container Orchestration Platforms
cat_sub22_sub5_sub1.description: Services for managing containerized applications and workloads on orchestration platforms like Kubernetes and its distributions.
cat_sub22_sub5_sub1.boundary: Not for general container runtimes (Docker) or specific application platforms (Heroku, Cloud Run).
cat_sub22_sub5_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages Kubernetes clusters, OpenShift, KubeSphere, or multi-cluster Kubernetes operations, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub5_sub13.name: Application Servers & Runtimes
cat_sub22_sub5_sub13.description: Services for managing WildFly, Oat++, and other application server runtimes.
cat_sub22_sub5_sub13.boundary: Not for container runtimes or cloud platforms.
cat_sub22_sub5_sub13.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages WildFly, Oat++, or similar application server runtimes, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub5_sub15.name: MLOps & AI Platforms
cat_sub22_sub5_sub15.description: Services for managing machine learning pipelines, model deployment, and AI platform operations.
cat_sub22_sub5_sub15.boundary: Not for general workflow orchestration or application platforms.
cat_sub22_sub5_sub15.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages ZenML, HuggingFace Spaces, or similar MLOps/AI platforms, classify here.

cat_sub22_sub5_sub2.name: Container Runtimes & Engines
cat_sub22_sub5_sub2.description: Services for managing container lifecycle, images, and runtime operations at the engine level.
cat_sub22_sub5_sub2.boundary: Not for container orchestration platforms (Kubernetes) or development containers (Devcontainer).

cat_sub24_sub20_sub14.name: Deep Research & Analysis
cat_sub24_sub20_sub14.description: Services that perform multi-step research, exploratory analysis, and complex information synthesis across multiple sources.
cat_sub24_sub20_sub14.boundary: Focus on multi-source synthesis and analysis. Involves automated research steps across domains. Excludes simple search or single-source retrieval.
cat_sub24_sub20_sub14.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily performs automated multi-step research, exploration, and synthesis across multiple sources/domains.

cat_sub24_sub20_sub15.name: AI Context Engineering & Knowledge Crystallization
cat_sub24_sub20_sub15.description: Services that transform repositories into optimized, AI-consumable knowledge through systematic analysis and context engineering.
cat_sub24_sub20_sub15.boundary: Focus on knowledge transformation for AI consumption. Involves systematic analysis, optimization, and context engineering of existing repositories.
cat_sub24_sub20_sub15.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily transforms existing knowledge repositories into optimized, structured context for AI systems through analysis and engineering.

cat_sub24_sub20_sub2.name: Web & General Search
cat_sub24_sub20_sub2.description: Services that perform broad web searches, extract content from websites, and provide general internet information retrieval.
cat_sub24_sub20_sub2.boundary: Focus on public web content and general-purpose search. Excludes specialized searches for structured data, code, or private knowledge.
cat_sub24_sub20_sub2.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily performs general-purpose web crawling, searching, or content extraction from public websites.

cat_sub24_sub20_sub4.name: Enterprise & Collaborative Knowledge
cat_sub24_sub20_sub4.description: Services that search structured enterprise data, business documents, internal wikis, and collaborative platforms.
cat_sub24_sub20_sub4.boundary: Focus on organizational and team knowledge. Includes JIRA, Notion, Confluence, and corporate wikis. Excludes personal files and public web.
cat_sub24_sub20_sub4.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily searches corporate documents, team wikis, project management tools, or enterprise knowledge graphs.

cat_sub24_sub20_sub5.name: Personal Knowledge & Files
cat_sub24_sub20_sub5.description: Services that search, organize, and retrieve information from personal documents, notes, ebooks, and local files.
cat_sub24_sub20_sub5.boundary: Focus on individual user content. Includes Markdown, PDFs, EPUBs, and local note collections. Excludes enterprise systems and chat logs.
cat_sub24_sub20_sub5.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily searches personal files, local documents, or individual note collections.

cat_sub24_sub20_sub7.name: Structured & Domain-Specific Data
cat_sub24_sub20_sub7.description: Services that search, retrieve, and analyze structured datasets from specific domains like finance, geospatial, or government.
cat_sub24_sub20_sub7.boundary: Focus on curated, domain-specific datasets. Includes financial markets, geospatial data, government open data, and public sector records.
cat_sub24_sub20_sub7.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily searches structured datasets from specific domains (finance, geography, government, etc.).

cat_sub24_sub20_sub8.name: Media & Published Content
cat_sub24_sub20_sub8.description: Services that search, retrieve, and analyze video content, articles, and published material from content platforms.
cat_sub24_sub20_sub8.boundary: Focus on published media and platform content. Includes YouTube videos, Substack articles, Medium posts, and similar platforms.
cat_sub24_sub20_sub8.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily searches, summarizes, or analyzes video content or published articles on content platforms.

cat_sub24_sub3_sub1.name: Code Generation & Writing
cat_sub24_sub3_sub1.description: Services that create, modify, generate, or scaffold source code, including templates and boilerplate.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub1.boundary: Focuses on producing or transforming source code. Excludes testing, debugging, and documentation generation.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily helps create, modify, generate, or scaffold source code, classify here.

cat_sub24_sub3_sub11.name: Context & AI Assistant Management
cat_sub24_sub3_sub11.description: Services that provide, manage, or structure context and knowledge specifically for AI coding assistants.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub11.boundary: Focuses on context for AI assistants. Excludes general documentation, repository search, and AI model management.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub11.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages context or knowledge specifically for AI coding assistants, classify here.

cat_sub24_sub3_sub12.name: Specialized Domain Development
cat_sub24_sub3_sub12.description: Services for development in specialized domains like electronics, games, mobile, or embedded systems.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub12.boundary: Focuses on non-web, specialized technical domains. Excludes general software development, web development, or API development.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub12.decision_rule: If the service primarily assists development in specialized technical domains (non-web), classify here.

cat_sub24_sub3_sub13.name: AI Model & Prompt Development
cat_sub24_sub3_sub13.description: Services that manage, evaluate, or optimize AI models, prompts, and AI-assisted workflows for developers.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub13.boundary: Focuses on the AI/ML components used in development. Excludes code generation, testing, and data science workflows.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub13.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages, evaluates, or optimizes AI models or prompts for development workflows, classify here.

cat_sub24_sub3_sub14.name: Data Science & ML Development
cat_sub24_sub3_sub14.description: Services for machine learning, data science, experiment tracking, and statistical analysis workflows.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub14.boundary: Focuses on ML/data science lifecycle. Excludes general software development, testing, and AI model management for coding.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub14.decision_rule: If the service primarily assists machine learning, data science, or statistical analysis development, classify here.

cat_sub24_sub3_sub15.name: Browser & Automation Tools
cat_sub24_sub3_sub15.description: Services that automate browser interactions, profiles, or DevTools for development, testing, or scraping.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub15.boundary: Focuses on browser automation and interaction. Excludes web application testing frameworks and general code execution.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub15.decision_rule: If the service primarily automates browser interactions, profiles, or DevTools for development tasks, classify here.

cat_sub24_sub3_sub16.name: Project & Workflow Management
cat_sub24_sub3_sub16.description: Services that help manage development projects, tasks, workflows, collaboration, and feature flags.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub16.boundary: Focuses on planning, tracking, and team coordination. Excludes code generation, testing, and repository analysis.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub16.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages development projects, tasks, workflows, or team collaboration, classify here.

cat_sub24_sub3_sub17.name: Development Workflow Platforms
cat_sub24_sub3_sub17.description: Integrated platforms that combine multiple AI-powered development capabilities into a unified workflow.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub17.boundary: Focuses on multi-functional platforms, not single-purpose tools. Excludes IDE integrations and single-function services.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub17.decision_rule: If the service is an integrated platform offering multiple combined AI development capabilities (e.g., code gen + testing + management), classify here.

cat_sub24_sub3_sub2.name: Testing & QA
cat_sub24_sub3_sub2.description: Services for automated testing, quality assurance, and test analysis for code, APIs, and web applications.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub2.boundary: Focuses on verifying functionality and quality. Excludes debugging, performance monitoring, and browser automation for non-testing purposes.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily performs testing, quality assurance, or test analysis (including API/web app testing), classify here.

cat_sub24_sub3_sub3.name: Debugging & Error Resolution
cat_sub24_sub3_sub3.description: Services that help identify, diagnose, and fix bugs, errors, or runtime issues in code.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub3.boundary: Focuses on problem diagnosis and fixing. Excludes general testing, code analysis for quality/security, and performance monitoring.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily helps find and fix bugs or runtime errors in code, classify here.

cat_sub24_sub3_sub4.name: Code Analysis & Review
cat_sub24_sub3_sub4.description: Services that analyze code for security, architecture, quality, compliance, or structural insights.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub4.boundary: Focuses on static or dynamic analysis for insights. Excludes testing, debugging, and documentation generation.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily analyzes code for insights, security, architecture, or quality, classify here.

cat_sub24_sub3_sub5.name: Documentation & Knowledge Management
cat_sub24_sub3_sub5.description: Services that create, manage technical documentation, or structure knowledge bases from code/repositories.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub5.boundary: Focuses on creating or organizing human-readable information. Excludes code analysis, repository search, and context management for AI assistants.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub5.decision_rule: If the service primarily creates, updates, or manages technical documentation or codebase knowledge bases, classify here.

cat_sub24_sub3_sub6.name: Repository Search & Navigation
cat_sub24_sub3_sub6.description: Services that enable searching, exploring, or navigating code repositories and codebases.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub6.boundary: Focuses on code discovery and navigation. Excludes documentation generation, code analysis, and project management.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub6.decision_rule: If the service primarily helps search, explore, or navigate code repositories, classify here.

cat_sub24_sub3_sub7.name: Development Environment Integration
cat_sub24_sub3_sub7.description: Services that integrate AI capabilities into specific IDEs, editors, or developer tools (e.g., VS Code, JetBrains).
cat_sub24_sub3_sub7.boundary: Focuses on integration with a specific development tool. Excludes general code execution, testing frameworks, and workflow platforms.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub7.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates AI with a specific IDE, code editor, or developer tool, classify here.

cat_sub24_sub3_sub8.name: Code Execution & Sandboxing
cat_sub24_sub3_sub8.description: Services that execute code in isolated environments, containers, or sandboxes for testing or runtime.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub8.boundary: Focuses on providing secure execution environments. Excludes debugging tools, testing frameworks, and IDE integrations.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub8.decision_rule: If the service primarily executes code in isolated environments or sandboxes, classify here.

cat_sub24_sub3_sub9.name: Performance & Optimization
cat_sub24_sub3_sub9.description: Services that monitor, analyze, or optimize application performance, runtime behavior, or resource usage.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub9.boundary: Focuses on runtime efficiency and optimization. Excludes debugging, functional testing, and code quality analysis.
cat_sub24_sub3_sub9.decision_rule: If the service primarily monitors, analyzes, or optimizes application performance or resource usage, classify here.

cat_sub24_sub4_sub1.name: Text Generation & Editing
cat_sub24_sub4_sub1.description: Services that create, edit, or rewrite prose content from scratch or minimal input.
cat_sub24_sub4_sub1.boundary: Generates new text content (articles, posts, copy) or performs substantive editing/rewriting. Excludes analysis of existing content.
cat_sub24_sub4_sub1.decision_rule: If the service's primary function is generating new prose or rewriting existing text into new content, classify here. NOT for analyzing, summarizing, or moderating existing content without generation.

cat_sub24_sub4_sub10.name: Content Enhancement & Moderation
cat_sub24_sub4_sub10.description: Services that analyze, improve, detect AI-use, or moderate existing content.
cat_sub24_sub4_sub10.boundary: Analyzes, summarizes, detects issues, or improves quality of existing content. Does not generate new primary content.
cat_sub24_sub4_sub10.decision_rule: If the service primarily analyzes, summarizes, detects AI, moderates, or optimizes existing content without creating new primary material, classify here. NOT for generating new articles or rewriting content.

cat_sub24_sub4_sub11.name: Multimedia & Video Generation
cat_sub24_sub4_sub11.description: Services that generate, edit, or convert across multiple content types in integrated workflows.
cat_sub24_sub4_sub11.boundary: Creates or edits video content, or integrates generation across modalities (text, image, audio, video).
cat_sub24_sub4_sub11.decision_rule: If the service generates video, edits video with AI features, or integrates multiple AI modalities (e.g., text-to-video with voiceovers), classify here. NOT for standalone image or audio generation.

cat_sub24_sub4_sub12.name: Platform & Workflow Orchestration
cat_sub24_sub4_sub12.description: Platforms to discover, manage, and run various AI models for content creation.
cat_sub24_sub4_sub12.boundary: Provides infrastructure, marketplaces, or orchestration for multiple AI tools. Does not create content directly.
cat_sub24_sub4_sub12.decision_rule: If the service primarily acts as a platform, hub, or orchestrator for discovering/combining other AI models, classify here. NOT for end-user content creation tools.

cat_sub24_sub4_sub2.name: Image Generation & Editing
cat_sub24_sub4_sub2.description: Services that create, modify, enhance, or vectorize 2D images from text or other images.
cat_sub24_sub4_sub2.boundary: Focuses on standalone 2D raster or vector images. Excludes 3D models and video frames.
cat_sub24_sub4_sub2.decision_rule: If the service's primary function is generating, editing, upscaling, or vectorizing standalone 2D images, classify here. NOT for image generation as part of 3D models or videos.

cat_sub24_sub4_sub4.name: Audio & Speech Generation
cat_sub24_sub4_sub4.description: Services that synthesize speech, clone voices, or generate music and sound effects.
cat_sub24_sub4_sub4.boundary: Focuses on audio generation. Excludes audio analysis or extraction from other media.
cat_sub24_sub4_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily generates or clones speech, voiceovers, or musical audio, classify here. NOT for services where audio is a secondary component of video creation.

cat_sub24_sub4_sub5.name: 3D & Model Creation
cat_sub24_sub4_sub5.description: Services that generate, edit, or manipulate 3D models, CAD files, or 3D scenes.
cat_sub24_sub4_sub5.boundary: Focuses on 3D assets and scene data. Excludes 2D images of 3D subjects.
cat_sub24_sub4_sub5.decision_rule: If the service primarily creates or edits 3D models, CAD files, or 3D scene data, classify here. NOT for services generating 2D images or videos.

cat_sub24_sub4_sub6.name: Document & Structured Content
cat_sub24_sub4_sub6.description: Services that create, edit, or process structured documents, presentations, and business content.
cat_sub24_sub4_sub6.boundary: Works with multi-page documents, presentations, resumes, and structured business content.
cat_sub24_sub4_sub6.decision_rule: If the service primarily works with document formats (PDF, PPT), creates presentations, or generates structured business content (resumes, reports), classify here. NOT for free-form text generation.

cat_sub28_sub1_sub1.name: General Web Search
cat_sub28_sub1_sub1.description: Services providing broad internet search across all content types, returning general web results.
cat_sub28_sub1_sub1.boundary: Excludes specialized search engines for specific content types (news, shopping, academic) and web scraping/extraction tools.
cat_sub28_sub1_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides general-purpose web search across all topics, classify here.

cat_sub28_sub1_sub11.name: SEO & Keyword Research
cat_sub28_sub1_sub11.description: Services focused on search engine optimization data, keyword analysis, and ranking information.
cat_sub28_sub1_sub11.boundary: Excludes general web search and services that don't provide SEO-specific metrics.
cat_sub28_sub1_sub11.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides SEO data, keyword research, or ranking analysis, classify here.

cat_sub28_sub1_sub15.name: Conversational AI Search
cat_sub28_sub1_sub15.description: Services optimized for AI agents and conversational interfaces with enhanced context understanding.
cat_sub28_sub1_sub15.boundary: Excludes traditional search engines not specifically designed for AI/agent use.
cat_sub28_sub1_sub15.decision_rule: If the service is specifically designed for AI agents or conversational interfaces, classify here.

cat_sub28_sub1_sub16.name: Document Conversion & Processing
cat_sub28_sub1_sub16.description: Services that fetch web content and convert it to different formats like Markdown or structured data.
cat_sub28_sub1_sub16.boundary: Excludes search engines that don't convert content and services focused only on extraction.
cat_sub28_sub1_sub16.decision_rule: If the service primarily converts web content to different formats, classify here.

cat_sub28_sub1_sub17.name: Multi-Source Aggregation
cat_sub28_sub1_sub17.description: Services that aggregate results from multiple search engines or data sources.
cat_sub28_sub1_sub17.boundary: Excludes single-source search engines and services that don't aggregate multiple sources.
cat_sub28_sub1_sub17.decision_rule: If the service primarily aggregates results from multiple search engines, classify here.

cat_sub28_sub1_sub19.name: Browser Automation
cat_sub28_sub1_sub19.description: Services that automate browser interactions for testing, scraping, or interaction purposes.
cat_sub28_sub1_sub19.boundary: Excludes search engines and services that don't involve browser automation.
cat_sub28_sub1_sub19.decision_rule: If the service primarily automates browser interactions, classify here.

cat_sub28_sub1_sub2.name: Web Scraping & Extraction
cat_sub28_sub1_sub2.description: Services focused on extracting specific content or data from websites, often bypassing anti-bot measures.
cat_sub28_sub1_sub2.boundary: Excludes general search engines that return links/snippets and services focused only on converting page formats.
cat_sub28_sub1_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily extracts structured data or content from specific web pages, classify here.

cat_sub28_sub1_sub3.name: Search Engine Indexing
cat_sub28_sub1_sub3.description: Services for building, maintaining, or querying search engine indexes and databases.
cat_sub28_sub1_sub3.boundary: Excludes general web search APIs and specialized search services for specific domains.
cat_sub28_sub1_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides infrastructure for creating or querying search indexes, classify here.

cat_sub4_sub22_sub1.name: Payment Processing & Gateways
cat_sub4_sub22_sub1.description: Services for processing online payments, handling transactions, and managing payment gateways for e-commerce and applications.
cat_sub4_sub22_sub1.boundary: Not for banking account management (see Banking & Accounts) or cryptocurrency-native payments (see Cryptocurrency Payments).
cat_sub4_sub22_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables accepting, processing, or managing traditional (fiat) electronic payments for goods/services, classify here.

cat_sub4_sub22_sub10.name: Algorithmic Trading
cat_sub4_sub22_sub10.description: Services for designing, backtesting, and executing automated trading strategies and quantitative models across markets.
cat_sub4_sub22_sub10.boundary: Not for manual trade execution interfaces (see Trading & Brokerage) or raw market data (see Market Data & Feeds).
cat_sub4_sub22_sub10.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables the creation, testing, or automated execution of programmed trading strategies, classify here.

cat_sub4_sub22_sub11.name: E-commerce Platforms
cat_sub4_sub22_sub11.description: Services for managing online storefronts, including products, orders, customers, and inventory for merchant businesses.
cat_sub4_sub22_sub11.boundary: Not for payment processing (see Payment Processing) or advertising management (see Advertising Platforms).
cat_sub4_sub22_sub11.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides API access to manage the core operations of an online store or marketplace, classify here.

cat_sub4_sub22_sub14.name: Advertising Platforms
cat_sub4_sub22_sub14.description: Services for managing ad campaigns, retrieving performance analytics, and optimizing spending on digital advertising platforms.
cat_sub4_sub22_sub14.boundary: Not for e-commerce store management (see E-commerce Platforms) or general payment processing (see Payment Processing).
cat_sub4_sub22_sub14.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides API access to create, manage, or analyze advertising campaigns and spend, classify here.

cat_sub4_sub22_sub17.name: Crypto Analytics & On-chain Intel
cat_sub4_sub22_sub17.description: Services providing analytics, behavioral prediction, fraud detection, and specialized intelligence focused on blockchain and cryptocurrency activity.
cat_sub4_sub22_sub17.boundary: Not for general blockchain data access (see Blockchain Infrastructure) or DeFi operations (see Decentralized Finance (DeFi)).
cat_sub4_sub22_sub17.decision_rule: If the service primarily analyzes on-chain behavior, wallet patterns, or provides crypto-specific risk/fraud intelligence, classify here.

cat_sub4_sub22_sub18.name: Digital Wallet Management
cat_sub4_sub22_sub18.description: Services focused on the management, tracking, and operations of cryptocurrency wallets, including allowances, revocations, and multi-chain support.
cat_sub4_sub22_sub18.boundary: Not for DeFi protocol interactions (see Decentralized Finance (DeFi)) or crypto payments (see Cryptocurrency Payments).
cat_sub4_sub22_sub18.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages the state, permissions, or assets within a cryptocurrency wallet, classify here.

cat_sub4_sub22_sub19.name: Multi-chain & Cross-chain
cat_sub4_sub22_sub19.description: Services that abstract or facilitate operations across multiple blockchain networks, enabling cross-chain interactions and strategy execution.
cat_sub4_sub22_sub19.boundary: Not for single-chain DeFi (see Decentralized Finance (DeFi)) or single blockchain infrastructure (see Blockchain Infrastructure).
cat_sub4_sub22_sub19.decision_rule: If the service's primary function is to enable or simplify operations that span multiple different blockchain networks, classify here.

cat_sub4_sub22_sub2.name: Banking & Accounts
cat_sub4_sub22_sub2.description: Services for accessing and managing traditional bank accounts, balances, transactions, and personal/business banking data.
cat_sub4_sub22_sub2.boundary: Not for payment processing (see Payment Processing) or investment/trading accounts (see Trading & Brokerage).
cat_sub4_sub22_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides API access to manage checking/savings accounts or transactions at a traditional bank or neobank, classify here.

cat_sub4_sub22_sub3.name: Trading & Brokerage
cat_sub4_sub22_sub3.description: Services for executing trades, managing investment portfolios, and accessing brokerage accounts for stocks, options, and other traditional securities.
cat_sub4_sub22_sub3.boundary: Not for cryptocurrency trading (see Cryptocurrency Exchanges) or raw market data feeds (see Market Data & Feeds).
cat_sub4_sub22_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables buying/selling traditional securities (stocks, options, ETFs) or managing a brokerage account, classify here.

cat_sub4_sub22_sub4.name: Cryptocurrency Exchanges
cat_sub4_sub22_sub4.description: Services for trading cryptocurrencies, accessing exchange wallets, and managing spot/margin trading on centralized crypto platforms.
cat_sub4_sub22_sub4.boundary: Not for on-chain DeFi interactions (see Decentralized Finance (DeFi)) or crypto payment processing (see Cryptocurrency Payments).
cat_sub4_sub22_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides API access to a centralized cryptocurrency exchange for trading or account management, classify here.

cat_sub4_sub22_sub5.name: Decentralized Finance (DeFi)
cat_sub4_sub22_sub5.description: Services for interacting with on-chain DeFi protocols, including token swaps, liquidity provisioning, lending, and staking across blockchains.
cat_sub4_sub22_sub5.boundary: Not for centralized exchange trading (see Cryptocurrency Exchanges) or general blockchain data/exploration (see Blockchain Infrastructure).
cat_sub4_sub22_sub5.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables programmatic interaction with smart contracts for DeFi operations (swaps, lending, staking), classify here.

cat_sub4_sub22_sub6.name: Cryptocurrency Payments
cat_sub4_sub22_sub6.description: Services for accepting, processing, and managing cryptocurrency as a payment method for goods and services.
cat_sub4_sub22_sub6.boundary: Not for on-chain DeFi trading (see Decentralized Finance (DeFi)) or general crypto wallet/blockchain tools (see Blockchain Infrastructure).
cat_sub4_sub22_sub6.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables merchants to accept crypto payments or facilitates crypto-based checkout flows, classify here.

cat_sub4_sub22_sub7.name: Blockchain Infrastructure
cat_sub4_sub22_sub7.description: Services providing foundational access to blockchain networks, data, wallets, and core on-chain operations like token minting or transfers.
cat_sub4_sub22_sub7.boundary: Not for specific DeFi protocol interactions (see Decentralized Finance (DeFi)) or crypto exchange trading (see Cryptocurrency Exchanges).
cat_sub4_sub22_sub7.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides generalized read/write access to blockchain data, networks, or core wallet/token functions, classify here.

cat_sub4_sub22_sub9.name: Analytics & Intelligence
cat_sub4_sub22_sub9.description: Services providing advanced financial analysis, forecasting, risk assessment, and data-driven insights for investment or business decisions.
cat_sub4_sub22_sub9.boundary: Not for raw market data feeds (see Market Data & Feeds) or automated trade execution (see Algorithmic Trading).
cat_sub4_sub22_sub9.decision_rule: If the service primarily performs analytical computations, generates forecasts, or provides specialized intelligence on financial data, classify here.

cat_sub6_sub18_sub1.name: Customer Relationship Management
cat_sub6_sub18_sub1.description: Services for integrating with CRM platforms to manage customer data, sales pipelines, and marketing activities.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub1.boundary: Not for general business data platforms (Windsor) or project management tools (Linear). Focuses on dedicated CRM systems.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates with a dedicated CRM platform like Salesforce or HubSpot, classify here.

cat_sub6_sub18_sub10.name: Unified Business Data
cat_sub6_sub18_sub10.description: Services for aggregating and querying data from multiple business sources into a single accessible layer.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub10.boundary: Not for direct integration with a single primary application (CRM, ERP). Focuses on data federation across many sources.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub10.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides a unified query interface across many integrated SaaS tools (Windsor, Nerve, Boost.space), classify here.

cat_sub6_sub18_sub11.name: Workflow Automation Platforms
cat_sub6_sub18_sub11.description: Services for integrating with platforms that enable the design, execution, and monitoring of automated workflows across apps.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub11.boundary: Not for the business applications being connected (CRM, ERP). Focuses on the automation orchestration engine itself.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub11.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates with a workflow automation engine like n8n, Simplifier, or iFlytek Workflow, classify here.

cat_sub6_sub18_sub12.name: Low-Code/No-Code Platforms
cat_sub6_sub18_sub12.description: Services for connecting to platforms that enable application development and process automation with minimal coding.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub12.boundary: Not for the end-user SaaS tools. Focuses on the development/automation platform used to build integrations.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub12.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates with a low-code/no-code development environment (Fibery, Simplifier as a platform), classify here.

cat_sub6_sub18_sub16.name: Customer Support & Helpdesk
cat_sub6_sub18_sub16.description: Services for integrating with platforms that manage customer support tickets, helpdesk interactions, and service requests.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub16.boundary: Not for general project management or communication. Focuses specifically on customer issue tracking.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub16.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates with a customer support or ticketing system like Thena or Ticket-Generator, classify here.

cat_sub6_sub18_sub19.name: Multi-Application Connectors
cat_sub6_sub18_sub19.description: Services that provide pre-built connectors to a vast array of other SaaS applications through a single integration point.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub19.boundary: Not for unified data querying or workflow automation engines. Focuses on the connector aggregation service itself.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub19.decision_rule: If the service is primarily a gateway offering access to thousands of other app connectors (Knit, Maton, Integration App), classify here.

cat_sub6_sub18_sub2.name: Enterprise Resource Planning
cat_sub6_sub18_sub2.description: Services for connecting to ERP systems to manage core business processes like finance, supply chain, and manufacturing.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub2.boundary: Not for CRM, project management, or specialized scheduling systems. Focuses on comprehensive business management suites.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates with a comprehensive ERP system like NetSuite, Odoo, or D365FO, classify here.

cat_sub6_sub18_sub3.name: Project & Task Management
cat_sub6_sub18_sub3.description: Services for integrating with platforms that manage projects, tasks, tickets, and team collaboration workflows.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub3.boundary: Not for CRM, ERP, or communication tools. Focuses on work planning and execution tracking.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates with a project, task, or issue management system like Linear, Jira, or OpenProject, classify here.

cat_sub6_sub18_sub4.name: Communication Platforms
cat_sub6_sub18_sub4.description: Services for connecting to platforms that facilitate messaging, voice, video calls, and team communication.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub4.boundary: Not for project management or customer support ticketing. Focuses on real-time or asynchronous communication channels.
cat_sub6_sub18_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates with a communication tool like Slack, WhatsApp, Microsoft Teams, or telephony (Telnyx), classify here.

cat_sub7_sub1_sub1.name: Personal Task Management
cat_sub7_sub1_sub1.description: Services focused on managing individual to-do lists, personal reminders, and simple task lists for an individual user.
cat_sub7_sub1_sub1.boundary: Does not include services primarily for team collaboration, formal project structures, or specialized work items like software bugs or product features.
cat_sub7_sub1_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages simple, individual to-do lists and personal reminders, classify here.

cat_sub7_sub1_sub11.name: Database-Powered Work Management
cat_sub7_sub1_sub11.description: Services that use a flexible database/spreadsheet-like interface as the core model for creating and tracking tasks, projects, and custom work items.
cat_sub7_sub1_sub11.boundary: Does not include services with fixed, pre-defined data models for tasks or issues (e.g., classic project management tools).
cat_sub7_sub1_sub11.decision_rule: If the service primarily models tasks and projects using a user-definable database or spreadsheet paradigm, classify here.

cat_sub7_sub1_sub12.name: Operating System & Device Task Integration
cat_sub7_sub1_sub12.description: Services that manage tasks and reminders deeply integrated with a specific operating system or device ecosystem (e.g., native apps).
cat_sub7_sub1_sub12.boundary: Does not include cross-platform, cloud-based task management services.
cat_sub7_sub1_sub12.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages tasks by integrating with native OS services (e.g., system reminders, messages) on a specific platform, classify here.

cat_sub7_sub1_sub2.name: Team Project Management
cat_sub7_sub1_sub2.description: Services for coordinating collaborative work across teams using projects, boards, lists, and tasks with shared visibility.
cat_sub7_sub1_sub2.boundary: Does not include services built around formal issue/bug tracking, product roadmaps, or personal task lists without team features.
cat_sub7_sub1_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables team collaboration on tasks organized within projects or boards, classify here.

cat_sub7_sub1_sub3.name: Software Development & Issue Tracking
cat_sub7_sub1_sub3.description: Services designed to track software development work items like bugs, features, and tickets within a development lifecycle.
cat_sub7_sub1_sub3.boundary: Does not include general team project management, personal task managers, or product strategy platforms.
cat_sub7_sub1_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily tracks software bugs, development tickets, or issues within a dev workflow (e.g., sprints, backlogs), classify here.

cat_sub7_sub1_sub4.name: Product Development & Roadmapping
cat_sub7_sub1_sub4.description: Services for managing product discovery, strategy, and roadmaps by tracking features, opportunities, and customer feedback.
cat_sub7_sub1_sub4.boundary: Does not include general project management, software bug tracking, or task execution platforms.
cat_sub7_sub1_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages product strategy, feature roadmaps, or product discovery processes, classify here.

cat_sub7_sub1_sub6.name: Workflow & Process Automation
cat_sub7_sub1_sub6.description: Services that orchestrate and automate sequences of tasks or actions across different tools or within a platform.
cat_sub7_sub1_sub6.boundary: Does not include services primarily for manual task/project management without a core automation engine.
cat_sub7_sub1_sub6.decision_rule: If the service primarily automates task sequences or connects workflows across systems, classify here.

cat_sub7_sub1_sub7.name: Agent & AI Task Coordination
cat_sub7_sub1_sub7.description: Services specifically designed for AI agents or autonomous systems to collaborate, share context, and manage delegated tasks.
cat_sub7_sub1_sub7.boundary: Does not include traditional human-centric task/project management tools without specific AI-agent collaboration features.
cat_sub7_sub1_sub7.decision_rule: If the service is primarily a platform for AI agents to collaborate on and manage tasks, classify here.
